---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: ../course-outline.css
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    margin: 0.05
    footer: "<a href='javascript:history.back()'>← Back</a>"
---

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
  <h2>III. Jesus in Creation</h2>

<div class="lv-a"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a-m_6KN5ISA&list=RDa-m_6KN5ISA&start_radio=1" target="_blank">Opening Song</a></div>

<details>
  <summary><strong>Introduction — Why I Think This Matters</strong></summary>
  <img src="why people leave church.jpeg" alt="Why people leave church" style="max-width:100%; margin:1em 0;">
  <p>The lessons up to this point have focused on the core, non-negotiable doctrines of the Christian faith — beliefs like the deity of Christ, where we can say plainly: if you reject this, you are rejecting Christianity itself.</p>
  <p>Today's lesson is different. Some of the Scriptures we'll look at are interpreted differently by sincere, Bible-believing Christians, and I want to be upfront about that. I have come to believe in a young earth and a literal six-day creation. I'm also convinced that God did not use billions of years of death and suffering to create a world He called "very good." I hold these views with real conviction — but as my mother used to remind me, <em>"Bill, just remember you don't know everything."</em></p>
  <p>My goal today is not to win an argument. If you leave unconvinced of a young earth, I'd simply hope you could say: <em>"I can see how an intelligent, Bible-believing Christian might come to that conclusion."</em> Young earth believers are too often dismissed — even by fellow Christians — as naive or anti-intellectual. I hope this lesson pushes back on that a little, and that we can discuss it the way brothers and sisters in Christ should.</p>
</details>

<details>
  <summary><strong>A. Jesus in Creation from Scripture</strong></summary>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">1. Overview</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://bibleproject.com/videos/genesis-1/" target="_blank">Creation Video — Bible Project</a></div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">2. New Testament Passages</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A1-3&version=CSB" target="_blank">John 1:1-3</a> — The Word (Jesus) was with God in the beginning; all things were created through Him</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+1%3A15-17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Colossians 1:15-17</a> — Jesus is the image of the invisible God; all things created through Him and for Him; in Him all things hold together</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+1%3A1-3&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 1:1-3</a> — God created the world through His Son, who upholds the universe by the word of His power</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1Cor+8%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 8:6</a> — One Lord Jesus Christ, through whom are all things</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">e. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Eph+3%3A9&version=WEB" target="_blank">Ephesians 3:9</a> — God created all things (some manuscripts: "through Jesus Christ")</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">3. Old Testament Passages</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis%201-2%3A3&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 1-2:2</a> — "Let us make man in our image" — plural language hinting at plurality within God</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Proverbs+8%3A22-31&version=WEB" target="_blank">Proverbs 8:22-31</a> — Wisdom personified as present and active at creation (often interpreted as pointing to the pre-incarnate Christ)</div>
  </details>
</details>

<details>
  <summary><strong>B. Scriptures On Six Day Event and Man Made on Day Six</strong></summary>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">1. Six-Day Creation</summary>
    <details>
      <summary class="lv-lc">a. How long is a day?</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">i. Twenty-four hours</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">ii. Daytime (daylight hours)</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">iii. An era or period of time</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">iv. In my father's day, it could take three days to drive across Texas driving only during the day</div>
    </details>
    <details>
      <summary class="lv-lc">b. Scripture on days</summary>
      <details style="margin-left:2em">
        <summary class="lv-detail">i. Six-Day Creation Scripture References</summary>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A5-31&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 1:5–31</a> — "Morning and evening" — a day</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+20%3A8-11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 20:8–11</a> — Direct statement: creation in six days</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+31%3A17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 31:17</a> — Direct statement: creation in six days</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+4%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 4:4</a> — Reference to seventh-day rest</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+31%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 31:18</a> — "…the two tablets… written with the finger of God"</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+32%3A16&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 32:16</a> — "The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God"</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+9%3A10&version=WEB" target="_blank">Deuteronomy 9:10</a> — "…the two tablets of stone written with the finger of God"</div>
      </details>
    </details>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">2. Man Created in the Beginning (Day 6)</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Popular view: man appeared billions of years after creation via evolution</div>
    <details>
      <summary class="lv-lc">b. Scripture on man existing from the beginning</summary>
      <details style="margin-left:2em">
        <summary class="lv-detail">i. New Testament Scriptures</summary>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+10%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Mark 10:6</a> — "From the beginning of creation, God made them male and female"</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+19%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 19:4</a> — "From the beginning God made them male and female"</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+11%3A50-51&version=WEB" target="_blank">Luke 11:50–51</a> — Abel referenced from the foundation of the world</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+17%3A26&version=WEB" target="_blank">Acts 17:26</a> — Humanity descended from one man</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A45&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15:45</a> — Adam identified as the first man</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+4%3A3%3B+9%3A26&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 4:3; 9:26</a> — Events tied to the foundation of the world</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+13%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 13:8</a> — Redemptive plan from the foundation of the world</div>
      </details>
      <details style="margin-left:2em">
        <summary class="lv-detail">ii. Old Testament Scriptures</summary>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A26-27&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 1:26–27</a> — God made man on Day 6, in His image</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+2%3A7&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 2:7</a> — "the Lord God formed the man of dust from the ground"</div>
        <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+5%3A1-2&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 5:1–2</a> — "When God created man, he made him in the likeness of God"</div>
      </details>
    </details>
  </details>
</details>

<details>
  <summary><strong>C. God's Truthfulness and Biblical View of Death</strong></summary>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">1. God Cannot Lie or Deceive</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+6%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 6:18</a> — "it is impossible for God to lie"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+1%3A2&version=WEB" target="_blank">Titus 1:2</a> — "God, who never lies, promised before the ages began"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Numbers+23%3A19&version=WEB" target="_blank">Numbers 23:19</a> — "God is not man, that he should lie… Has he said, and will he not do it?"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Malachi+3%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Malachi 3:6</a> — "For I the Lord do not change"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">e. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+13%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 13:8</a> — "Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">f. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+1%3A17&version=WEB" target="_blank">James 1:17</a> — "with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">g. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+32%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Deuteronomy 32:4</a> — "A God of faithfulness and without iniquity, just and upright is he"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">h. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+3%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 3:4</a> — "Let God be true though every one were a liar"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">i. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Samuel+15%3A29&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Samuel 15:29</a> — "the Glory of Israel will not lie or have regret"</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">j. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Timothy+2%3A13&version=WEB" target="_blank">2 Timothy 2:13</a> — "he remains faithful — for he cannot deny himself"</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">2. God Did Not Use Death to Create</summary>
    <details style="margin-left:2em">
      <summary class="lv-lc">a. "Very Good" creation did not have death</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A31&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 1:31</a> — "God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Death and suffering are incompatible with a creation God declared "very good"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://bibleproject.com/videos/vocab-insight-tov-good/" target="_blank">Tov</a> — Bible Project video on the Hebrew word for "good"</div>
    </details>
    <details style="margin-left:2em">
      <summary class="lv-lc">b. Death entered through the Fall — Man's Rebellion</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+2%3A15-17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 2:15-17</a> — "in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A17-19&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 3:17–19</a> — curse on creation; "to dust you shall return"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+5%3A12&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 5:12</a> — "sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+6%3A23&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 6:23</a> — "the wages of sin is death"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A21-23&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15:21-23</a> — "by a man came death… by a man has come also the resurrection"</div>
    </details>
    <details style="margin-left:2em">
      <summary class="lv-lc">c. New Creation will have no death or suffering</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah%2025%3A8-9&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 25:8–9</a> — Death swallowed up</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Corinthians%2015%3A53%E2%80%9355&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15:53–55</a> — Mortal put on immortality</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+20%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 20:14</a> — Death thrown into the lake of fire</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+21%3A3-4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 21:3–4</a> — "He will wipe away every tear… death shall be no more, neither shall there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain anymore"</div>
    </details>
    <details style="margin-left:2em">
      <summary class="lv-lc">d. Death as enemy — not a creative tool</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A26&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15:26</a> — "The last enemy to be destroyed is death"</div>
    </details>
    <details style="margin-left:2em">
      <summary class="lv-lc">e. Jesus defeated death — He would not have designed creation through it</summary>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A25&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 11:25</a> — "I am the resurrection and the life"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Timothy+1%3A10&version=WEB" target="_blank">2 Timothy 1:10</a> — Christ "abolished death and brought life and immortality to light"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+2%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 2:14</a> — "through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death"</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 1:18</a> — "I have the keys of Death and Hades"</div>
    </details>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">3. God and Jesus Spoke as Though the Stories Were True</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Jesus referenced the beginning and Noah's flood as historical events</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+19%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 19:4</a> — "Have you not read that he who created them from the beginning made them male and female"</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+24%3A37-38&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 24:37–38</a> — "As were the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man"</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+17%3A26-27&version=WEB" target="_blank">Luke 17:26–27</a> — Noah's flood treated as literal historical event</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. All mentions of creation and the flood carry no hint of allegory or fable</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+5%3A12-14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 5:12–14</a> — Adam treated as a real historical person whose sin had real consequences</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+3%3A20&version=WEB" target="_blank">1 Peter 3:20</a> — Noah's flood referenced as historical fact</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Peter+3%3A5-6&version=WEB" target="_blank">2 Peter 3:5–6</a> — Creation and flood both affirmed as real events</div>
  </details>
</details>

<details>
  <summary><strong>D. Some Common Sense Readings of the Book of Nature</strong></summary>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">1. Creation of the Universe</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Time, matter, and space were all created together</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Einstein showed they are coupled and interdependent</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— E = MC² — mass and energy are interlinked</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Therefore time, space, and mass/energy came into existence at the same moment</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w6AHcv19NIc" target="_blank">Video: Time, Space, and Matter</a></div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. Whatever created the material world could not itself be material</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.youtube.com/shorts/saeW0lqCufM" target="_blank">Creator joke (clean version)</a></div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. Second Law of Thermodynamics</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Things do not naturally go from disorder to order</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Incredible complexity and order in the cosmos requires an explanation</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. Fine Tuning of the Universe</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Fine-tuning of physical constants points to intentional design</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=azBWPrgnyUk" target="_blank">Video: Fine Tuning of the Universe</a></div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">2. Creation of Life</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Life did not spring into existence from non-life</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— A "simple" cell is not simple</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— Information always comes from a mind — DNA and RNA are coded with information</div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D5g74JHmVHg" target="_blank">James Tour: Origin of Life Not Explained</a></div>
      <div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r4sP1E1Jd_Y" target="_blank">James Tour: Origin of Life a Problem of Time</a></div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">3. Creation of Diverse Life</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qxhuxg3WRfg" target="_blank">Coding of DNA</a></div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">4. Early Christians All Believed in a Young Earth</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Church fathers took creation literally</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— Basil the Great, Ambrose, Ephrem the Syrian — all interpreted the creation days as literal 24-hour days</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— No allegorical reading of Genesis 1 among the early fathers was motivated by science; figurative readings (e.g., Augustine) were based on literary, not scientific, concerns</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. Christian founders of modern science believed in a young earth</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">— Kepler, Newton, Faraday, Linnaeus — held to a recent creation consistent with Scripture</div>
  </details>
</details>

<details>
  <summary><strong>E. Physical Evidence Consistent with a Young Earth</strong></summary>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">1. Evidence for Noah's Flood and Rapid Geological Formations</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <strong>Ancient flood legends</strong>: Many cultures worldwide have flood legends remarkably similar to the account of Noah's Flood.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <strong>Early civilizations</strong>: Historical records and civilizations appear suddenly around 3000–4000 BC with advanced knowledge, with no evidence of millions of years of gradual primitive development.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <strong>Polystrate fossils</strong>: Trees and animal remains found vertically spanning multiple sedimentary layers supposedly deposited over millions of years.</div>
    <div class="lv-detail">i. <a href="jesus-in-creation_files/Polystrate.png" target="_blank">Example image</a></div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <strong>Fossil graveyards</strong>: Massive concentrations of rapidly buried creatures (e.g., dinosaur bone beds, fish shoals).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">e. <strong>Soft tissue in dinosaur fossils</strong>: Original proteins, blood vessels, and cells in bones dated to 65+ million years old (e.g., Mary Schweitzer's <em>T. rex</em> finds).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">f. <strong>Rapid canyon formation</strong>: Canyons (e.g., Mount St. Helens 1980 eruption) forming in days or weeks, not millions of years.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">g. <strong>Flat gaps / paraconformities</strong>: Missing supposed millions of years with little to no erosion between sedimentary layers. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Iqq-wPyEvoE&t=1s" target="_blank">Video</a></div>
    <div class="lv-lc">h. <strong>Great Missoula Flood</strong>: <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x6-W0eDsE3I" target="_blank">Long video</a> | <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gBvNVaPYmy8" target="_blank">Lake Missoula flood — Creationist interpretation</a></div>
    <div class="lv-lc">i. <strong>Bent rock layers without fracturing</strong>: Sedimentary strata tightly folded without the heat/pressure needed to deform hardened rock — suggesting layers were still soft when folded. <a href="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/55/Folding_of_alternate_layers_of_limestone_layers_with_chert_layers.jpg" target="_blank">Example image</a></div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">2. Radiometric Dating Issues</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <strong>Inconsistent dates</strong>: Different radiometric methods applied to the same rock often yield wildly different ages.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <strong>Excess helium in zircons</strong>: High helium levels from uranium decay suggest only thousands of years of decay rather than billions (RATE project).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <strong>Carbon-14 in "ancient" materials</strong>: Detectable C-14 in diamonds, coal, and fossils claimed to be millions of years old (C-14 half-life ~5,730 years limits detection to &lt;100,000 years).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <strong>Accelerated decay evidence</strong>: Proposals of rapid nuclear decay during the creation week or the Flood.</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">3. Biological and Genetic Evidence</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <strong>Human population growth</strong>: Current growth rates extrapolated backward fit a starting population of ~8 people (Noah's family) ~4,500 years ago.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <strong>Genetic entropy / mutation load</strong>: Accumulation of harmful mutations suggests genomes are degrading rapidly, inconsistent with deep time (John Sanford).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <strong>Living fossils</strong>: Species (e.g., coelacanth, horseshoe crab) essentially unchanged from fossils claimed to be hundreds of millions of years old.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <strong>Lack of transitional forms</strong>: Ongoing controversy over clear evolutionary intermediates in the fossil record.</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">4. Astronomical and Cosmological Arguments</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. <strong>Comet lifetimes</strong>: Short-period comets lose material quickly and should not exist after millions of years without a constant source (Oort cloud hypothesized but unobserved).</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. <strong>Magnetic field decay</strong>: Earth's magnetic field is decaying at a measurable rate; extrapolating backward, it would have been impossibly strong only tens of thousands of years ago.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">c. <strong>Spiral galaxy arms</strong>: Galaxies should wind up tightly after a few hundred million years, yet many retain clear spiral structure.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">d. <strong>Moon recession</strong>: The Moon is moving away from Earth; backward extrapolation suggests catastrophically close proximity within ~1–2 billion years (not 4.5 billion).</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">5. Salt in the Ocean</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Sodium input into the oceans from rivers exceeds removal rates. At current rates, the oceans should be much saltier if Earth were billions of years old.</div>
  </details>
  <details>
    <summary class="lv-1">6. Sediment and Erosion Rates</summary>
    <div class="lv-lc">a. Continents would erode down to sea level in roughly 10–20 million years at current erosion rates.</div>
    <div class="lv-lc">b. Ocean sediment thickness is far less than expected for hundreds of millions of years of accumulation.</div>
  </details>
</details>

</section>
```
